# Momenta’s flat HK debut tests appetite for China AV IPOs

Momenta's US$751m Hong Kong listing drew strong demand, but the stock's flat close raises questions about broader AV confidence. By Stewart Burnett

* By  [Stewart Burnett](https://www.automotiveworld.com/author/stewart-burnettautomotiveworld-com/)

Chinese autonomous driving firm Momenta has debuted on the Hong Kong stock exchange, pricing its IPO at the top of its range and raising HK$5.89bn (US$751m). However, the shares essentially traded flat on their 8 July debut, closing well off an early intraday high; the muted reception offers a window into ongoing investor appetite for autonomous vehicle (AV) listings amid an ongoing wave of Chinese smart technology listings in Hong Kong.

The stock opened at HK$301 against an offer price of HK$295.60, climbed as high as HK$314.80, then pulled back to trade around HK$299—a gain of little more than 1%. Dickie Wong, Executive Director of Research at uSMART, told *Reuters:* “July’s peak unlocking period is clearly weighing on things […] investors seem more selective on valuations right now, especially in AI or tech.”

Demand for the offering itself, however, was less up for debate. The retail tranche was oversubscribed more than 413 times, institutional orders topped HK$100bn, and cornerstone investors—which include [Mercedes-Benz](https://www.automotiveworld.com/news/mercedes-benz-momenta-hit-intelligent-driving-milestone/), BYD, SAIC, General Motors, BlackRock, GIC, and Fidelity International—cumulatively subscribed for roughly half the deal, taking the company’s valuation to about HK$70bn, some 50% above its last private funding round.

Founded in 2016 by former Microsoft researcher Cao Xudong, Suzhou-based Momenta supplies self-driving technology spanning SAE Levels 2 through 4 to automakers and ride-hailing platforms, including a system it developed for BYD’s [God’s Eye](https://www.automotiveworld.com/articles/byd-has-sold-one-million-gods-eye-equipped-evs/) assisted-driving feature. Nine of the world’s top ten automakers use its technology, which had been installed in well over one million vehicles ahead of the listing. The company also holds roughly 65% of the global market for externally-supplied urban navigation driver-assist systems.

Still, the financials explain some of the market’s caution. Revenue rose 82% year-on-year to CN¥2.41bn (US$355m) in 2025, but net losses widened 35% to CN¥3.46bn. The widening losses were attributed to increasing spend on research and development, which reached as research and development spending reached CN¥1.87bn or more than three-quarters of revenue. 

Momenta has said that it plans to use about 60% of the money it raised from the IPO listing for further investments into research and development. An additional 20% will be directed towards robotaxi deployment—it has [ongoing agreements](https://www.automotiveworld.com/articles/uber-and-momenta-to-begin-munich-robotaxi-testing-in-2026/) with Uber and Mercedes-Benz—as well as 10% for vehicle mass production and 10% for working capital. Chief Executive Cao Xudong has said that Momenta expects to reach profitability in 2028.

Gary Ng, senior economist for Asia Pacific at Natixis CIB, told *Reuters* that the lack of a sharp surge “suggests sentiment remains cautious with a focus on long-term opportunities, unlike the global AI hype”. This comes even as strong institutional demand pointed to genuine appetite for narratives surrounding China’s AI and AV leadership—albeit selectively. Reconova debuted the same day and dropped sharply, while BasicSemi rose. This suggests investors are differentiating by business model and near-term revenue visibility rather than applying a blanket discount to the AI category.

For direct rivals in self-driving like Pony AI and WeRide, which have [already tapped](https://www.automotiveworld.com/topics/autonomous-driving/pony-ai-and-weride-gain-hong-kong-listing-approval/) Hong Kong’s capital markets, Momenta’s tepid trading debut suggests investors are now differentiating for a credible, near-term path to profitability.
